label: Kyiv
sub: Capital city; survived 2022 siege; sustained missile/drone strikes throughout war

label: Kharkiv
sub: Ukraine's 2nd city; survived 2022 capture attempt; near active frontline; under daily Russian shelling

label: Mariupol
sub: Besieged and occupied May 2022; Azovstal defenders' last stand; city largely destroyed

label: Bakhmut
sub: Site of war's longest, bloodiest battle (Aug 2022–May 2023); captured by Wagner Group

label: Avdiivka
sub: Ukrainian stronghold since 2014; fell to Russia Feb 18, 2024 — Russia's biggest gain of 2024

label: Kherson
sub: Regional capital; liberated Nov 11, 2022; still shelled from Russian positions across Dnipro

label: Zaporizhzhia
sub: Frontline regional center; under regular Russian missile and drone attacks

label: Zaporizhzhia NPP
sub: Europe's largest nuclear plant; Russian-occupied since Mar 4, 2022; 6 reactors shut down; IAEA mission ongoing

label: Odesa
sub: Key Black Sea port city; major Russian strike target; critical for Ukrainian grain exports; protected by air defense

label: Dnipro
sub: Major industrial city; key logistics hub; sustained major missile strikes including Jan 2023 apartment building attack

label: Kramatorsk
sub: Administrative center of Ukrainian-held Donetsk Oblast; regular Russian strike target; railroad hub

label: Izyum
sub: Key Russian logistics hub liberated Sep 2022 counteroffensive; mass graves found post-liberation

label: Melitopol
sub: Russian-controlled city; key hub on land corridor to Crimea; primary target of failed 2023 counteroffensive

label: Bucha
sub: Site of documented Russian war crimes; hundreds of civilian bodies found after Russian withdrawal Apr 2022

label: Soledar
sub: Salt-mining town captured by Wagner Jan 2023; precursor to Bakhmut fall

label: Kakhovka Dam
sub: Destroyed Jun 6, 2023; caused catastrophic flooding; 1M+ lost water access; Russia accused of sabotage

label: Kerch Bridge
sub: Partially destroyed by Ukraine Oct 8, 2022 and Oct 2023; key supply route to occupied Crimea

label: Sevastopol
sub: Russian Black Sea Fleet HQ in occupied Crimea; targeted by Ukrainian drone and missile strikes throughout war

label: Tokmak
sub: Key Russian logistics hub in occupied Zaporizhzhia; primary HIMARS strike target; goal of 2023 Ukrainian counteroffensive

label: Pokrovsk
sub: Critical Ukrainian logistics hub in Donetsk; under intense Russian pressure since 2024; key to Ukrainian supply lines

label: Lyman
sub: Railroad hub liberated by Ukraine in Sep 2022 Kharkiv counteroffensive; key rail hub for Donbas

label: Lviv
sub: Key western logistics hub for NATO weapons deliveries; Polish border crossing point; major refugee transit city

label: Orikhiv
sub: Frontline town in Zaporizhzhia Oblast; focal point of 2023 Ukrainian counteroffensive breakthrough attempts

label: Dnipro River Line
sub: Active front along Dnipro River east of Kherson; Ukraine holds west bank; Russia east bank since Nov 2022

label: Chasiv Yar
sub: Key elevated position near Bakhmut; under intense Russian pressure since 2024; controls approaches to Kramatorsk

label: Mykolaiv
sub: Southern city; defended Ukrainian flank protecting Odesa; sustained Russian strikes throughout 2022

label: Sumy
sub: Border city; under frequent Russian cross-border shelling; Ukrainian forces used Sumy as staging for Kursk incursion

label: Donetsk City
sub: Russian-controlled administrative center of occupied Donetsk Oblast; occupied since 2014; front lines 10–15 km from city center

label: Kharkiv-Belgorod Front
sub: Active Russian cross-border pressure zone; Russia opened offensive from Belgorod into Kharkiv Oblast May 2024

label: Polish Border Crossing
sub: Primary NATO weapons and aid entry point into Ukraine; Medyka/Shehyni crossings handle bulk of military deliveries

label: Patriot Air Defense
sub: Patriot PAC-3 batteries positioned to defend Kyiv; successfully intercepted Kinzhal hypersonic missiles in May 2023

label: Snake Island
sub: Small island in Black Sea; captured by Russia Feb 2022; retaken by Ukraine Aug 7, 2022; site of famous Ukrainian defiance

label: Vuhledar
sub: Strategic mining town; Ukraine inflicted major Russian armored losses in early 2023; fell to Russia Oct 2024

label: Krasnohorivka
sub: Frontline town near Donetsk; contested throughout 2023–2024; Russia captured portions in 2024

label: Luhansk City
sub: Administrative center of Russian-occupied Luhansk Oblast; held by Russia since 2014

label: Kherson Oblast Front
sub: Southern front along Dnipro River; Ukraine holds Kherson city; Russia holds east bank including Oleshky

label: Kharkiv Aid Hub
sub: UNHCR and international NGO coordination hub for northeast Ukraine; critical humanitarian logistics center

label: Crimea Peninsula
sub: Russian-occupied since 2014; formally annexed; HQ of Black Sea Fleet; targeted by Ukrainian long-range strikes

label: Lipetsk Air Base (Russia)
sub: Russian air base targeted by Ukrainian long-range drones in 2024; housed Su-34 aircraft used for strikes

label: Sudzha (Russia – Kursk)
sub: Largest town captured by Ukraine in Aug 2024 Kursk incursion; recaptured by Russia Mar 2025

label: Izmail Port
sub: Danube river port; Russia attacked grain infrastructure here after withdrawing from Black Sea grain deal Jul 2023
